explanations.5: CONTAINMENT = NONE means the database won't use features that make it portable between SQL Server instances; it's the standard setting.
explanations.7: SIZE sets the initial storage space to 8192 kilobytes, MAXSIZE allows it to grow unlimited, and FILEGROWTH means it expands by 65536KB chunks each time.
explanations.8: LOG ON tells SQL Server where to store the transaction log file (the .ldf file), which records all changes so the database can recover from crashes.
explanations.9: The log file starts smaller but caps at 2048GB; this limits how much space the transaction log can consume.
explanations.10: CATALOG_COLLATION = DATABASE_DEFAULT means all objects in the database will use the same sorting/comparison rules as the database itself.
explanations.19: ON [PRIMARY] specifies that this table's data will be stored in the primary filegroup; it's usually unnecessary because PRIMARY is the default.
explanations.22: This inserts two rows at once by listing multiple value sets separated by commas, rather than running two separate INSERT statements.
